hypothesis: PR #1758 seed (CaseOps + PreQuant TTT LR=1e-3 Unfrozen, 3-seed claim)

Short-to-long seq-len curriculum: train at seq_len=1024 for first 25% of wallclock, then step to 2048 — enables ~2.5x faster early steps (quadratically cheaper attention) yielding ~35% more optimizer steps in the same 600s budget, aligned with SkyLadder-style context-window scheduling gains.

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian: pre-collect calibration batches, count token frequencies, give top-100 most-frequent tokens sqrt(2) activation scaling so their contribution to H=X^T X is 2x larger, biasing GPTQ error compensation toward the tokens that dominate BPB.

Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 0.01 to 0.005 (less regularization bias in H inversion) and block_size from 128 to 64 (finer per-block error propagation), both improving post-quantization fidelity at no time cost.

Two-tier frequency weighting: top-50 tokens get 2.0 activation weight (4× Hessian) and tokens 51-200 get sqrt(2) (2× Hessian), replacing the flat top-100 sqrt(2) scheme — stronger Hessian emphasis on the highest-BPB-impact tokens.
Split Muon weight decay by parameter type: attn matrices keep muon_wd=0.095, MLP matrices raised to muon_wd_mlp=0.115, applying stronger regularization where MLP layers benefit more from it.
ResFormer value-residual: layer-0 V mixed at fixed 0.5 into V of every later attention (zero added params, residual-topology change).
Raise TTT cosine LR floor from 0 to 0.5*ttt_lr so late chunks still adapt at 50% of peak instead of decaying to ~0.
Learnable per-layer ResFormer V-residual: each block adds nn.Parameter scalar v_alpha; v <- sigmoid(v_alpha)*V_1 + (1-sigmoid(v_alpha))*V_n with v_alpha init=0 (sigmoid=0.5).

Add nesterov=True to TTT SGD optimizer (momentum=0.9): lookahead-gradient step gives ~5-15% effective convergence speedup at zero compute cost, compounding exp_005's raised cosine LR floor on under-adapted late chunks.

MLP-only TTT: freeze attention+embedding weights, adapt only MLP fc/proj/norm/scale parameters (filter `'mlp' in name`) — more stable and cheaper per step (skips ~44 attention weight-gradient GEMMs, halves allreduce traffic), motivated by TTT-E2E (arxiv:2512.23675).

Replace 4-class word-position embedding (cummax scan) with 2-class binary word-type embedding (word-start vs continuation) via direct boolean lookup — eliminates sequential cummax overhead that caused eval_budget_overrun while preserving word-boundary signal at the input layer.

Learnable per-layer ResFormer V-residual: blocks 1..N-1 each carry an nn.Parameter scalar v_alpha; v <- sigmoid(v_alpha)*V_1 + (1-sigmoid(v_alpha))*V_n with v_alpha init=0 (sigmoid=0.5). Block 0 has no v_alpha (v_first=None there) to avoid DDP unused-param.
Cache word_start_mask at module level so the second deserialize() call (for TTT) skips the SPM vocab scan, saving ~4-5s of eval init overhead that caused the eval_budget_overrun.

Add per-feature embedding layer-scale (embed_norm_scale, [512], init=0) applied as x*(1+scale) after embedding RMSNorm — a fast-converging handle on per-dimension amplification that complements the slow 8192×512 tok_emb matrix.
Split Muon into two param groups — attention matrices use muon_wd=0.095, MLP matrices use muon_wd_mlp=0.115 — restoring the multi_596 regularization split that was absent from the PR#1758 seed.

Replace global SGD optimizer with fresh-per-chunk AdamW (betas=(0.9,0.95), wd=0.01) in TTT loop to get clean per-document adaptive step sizes and prevent momentum bleed-over between heterogeneous val chunks.
Replace cosine LR decay in TTT with flat LR (h.ttt_lr throughout all chunks) so later chunks get equal adaptation budget instead of near-zero LR.

Add word-position embedding (4 entries, shape [4,512]) that encodes within-word BPE subword depth (0=word-start, 1=2nd subword, 2=3rd, 3=4th+) computed via torch.cummax on SP leading-space mask, added to token embeddings before RMSNorm.

Add deep supervision auxiliary LM loss (coef=0.1) at encoder-decoder boundary: projects encoder final hidden state through tied embeddings to give direct language-modeling gradient signal to early layers, improving gradient flow for the undertrained model without any new artifact parameters.

Short-to-long seq-len curriculum (1024→2048 at frac=0.25) with warmup pre-compilation of the 1024 shape to avoid mid-training distributed recompile hazard causing NCCL timeout (fixed crash from exp_001).
MLP/attn Muon WD split (attn=0.095, mlp=0.115) plus ttt_epochs reduced 3→2 to clear eval budget; first submit showed val_bpb=1.080 but DQ_EVAL by 3.4s.
Split Muon MLP weight decay (muon_wd=0.095 attn, muon_wd_mlp=0.115 MLP) + reduce ttt_epochs from 3 to 2 to stay within the 600s eval budget.

Muon+ col-then-row post-normalization after Newton-Schulz orthogonalization (paper arxiv:2602.21545): normalizes each column then each row of the update matrix to unit norm, ensuring uniform per-neuron update magnitudes for rectangular MLP/attn weight matrices on top of existing MuonEq-R pre-normalization.
Compose exp_005's TTT cosine LR floor (0.5*ttt_lr instead of 0) on top of exp_014's WD split — restoring an independent keep that was lost when exp_014 forked from exp_000 instead of exp_005.

Late-training weight-decay taper: linearly reduce all optimizer WDs from 100% to 50% over the final 30% of training (frac 0.70→1.0), letting the model optimize more freely during warmdown when LR is already decaying.

Widen eval stride 64→128 (halves scoring forward-pass count, −3% context per position) and reinvest freed eval budget into ttt_epochs 2→4, recovering the original 4-epoch TTT quality within ~440s total eval time.
Focal loss (γ=1.0) with per-batch normalization: replace CE mean with (1-p_t)/mean(1-p_t) * nll, redistributing gradient budget toward hard tokens while keeping average loss scale equal to CE.

Bifurcate per-head q_gain into [H, 2]: col-0 scales the RoPE-rotated head-dim slice (positional channel), col-1 scales the NoPE pass-through slice (content channel). Both columns init at qk_gain_init=5.25 so step-0 is bit-equivalent to baseline.

Hybrid RoPE/NoPE layer pattern: late-decoder blocks 6, 8, 10 set rope_dims=0 (skip rotation entirely → NoPE), creating an alternating NoPE/RoPE stripe through the deep decoder; looped blocks 3-5 keep partial-RoPE for stable positional recurrence. Per Yang et al. 2025 (2501.18795) NoPE layers naturally specialize for long-range content retrieval while RoPE layers handle local recency; mixing them outperforms uniform partial-RoPE.
Per-layer TTT LR scaling: blocks 0–10 get LR scaled from 0.25× to 1.75× of ttt_lr (linear by depth), non-block params (embeddings, norms) keep 1.0×, cosine decay applied proportionally per group.

Reduce GPTQ error-propagation block_size from 128 to 32, giving 4× more frequent Hinv correction flushes and lower accumulated quantization error, with zero artifact-size impact (per-row scales are block_size-independent).

Lower peak Muon momentum from 0.99 to 0.95 (canonical modded-nanogpt value): velocity timescale shortens from ~e^(-step/100) to e^(-step/20), giving late-warmdown steps more on-policy recent-gradient signal instead of stale accumulated velocity over the long 72% cosine warmdown.

Set min_lr=0.10 so the linear warmdown LR floor is 10% of peak (not 0), preventing completely frozen-gradient final steps and letting the EMA capture meaningful training signal through end of 600s budget.
Raise min_lr from 0.10 to 0.15: extends the LR floor phase from ~43s to ~64s of training, keeping Muon updates at 15% of peak in the final warmdown stretch.

Replace fixed Newton-Schulz coefficients (3.4445, -4.775, 2.0315) with Polar Express per-iteration minimax-optimized coefficients (ICLR 2026), which converge to the polar factor ~2x faster in the same 5 NS steps, giving Muon higher-quality gradient orthogonalization throughout training.

Raise warmdown LR floor min_lr=0.10→0.15: exp_045 just established that a non-zero floor compounds well in our undertrained 600s regime (Δ=-0.001160 going from 0.0→0.10), but the curve was tested only at one point — testing 0.15 brackets which side of the peak we're on, with priors that Cerebras D2Z (arxiv 2502.15938) claims zero floor is generally optimal for under-trained models so the curve must peak then decline.

Raise muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.33→0.45: extends momentum ramp from 198s to 270s, keeping Muon momentum closer to start (0.92) longer so on-policy gradient signal dominates during early warmdown (warmdown starts at 168s, so 102s of overlap where LR is decaying AND momentum is still ramping — the fine-tuning sweet spot).

Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (min_lr=0.15 floor preserved): with linear decay LR hits the floor at frac≈0.892 (~64s before end), wasting learning signal; cosine decay stays above the floor until frac=1.0 exactly, keeping LR higher throughout warmdown for more gradient signal while converging to the same min_lr endpoint.

Raise muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.45→0.55: extend Muon momentum ramp 270s→330s, increasing warmdown-overlap-with-ramping-momentum from 102s→162s — pushes the same axis exp_064 just kept (0.20 discard, 0.45 keep, longer-ramp-is-better trend).
Add weight_decay=0.1 to TTT SGD optimizer: regularizes chunk-to-chunk weight drift, preventing early-chunk over-adaptation from degrading later-chunk predictions.

Add a learnable [model_dim=512] word-start structural embedding (zero-init) that is added to the post-RMSNorm input representation for all word-initial tokens (those whose SP piece starts with ▁), encoding the linguistic word-boundary prior explicitly so the transformer can focus embedding capacity on token-specific content rather than re-learning the shared word-start bias from co-occurrence patterns.

2-step-ahead auxiliary CE loss (α=0.15): at position t, co-train the same tied-embedding projection to predict x_{t+2} in addition to x_{t+1}, adding a richer look-ahead gradient signal with zero extra artifact parameters.

Frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: pre-collect all calibration batches, count token frequencies, boost top-100 most-frequent tokens by sqrt(2) in H=X^TX, biasing GPTQ to minimize quantization error on common tokens. This technique improved bpb by -0.001812 on the old baseline (exp_002) but was never merged into the main lineage.

Per-chunk NLL-adaptive TTT learning rate: after score-first evaluation of each chunk, scale the cosine-scheduled adaptation LR by clip(chunk_nll/running_mean_nll, 0.5, 2.0), reallocating adaptation budget toward chunks where the model is struggling most.
Raise ema_decay 0.9965→0.998: extends EMA half-life 198→346 steps to better average the long warmdown_frac=0.72 trajectory now that min_lr=0.15 produces stable, meaningfully-moving late steps.

Nesterov SGD + per-chunk momentum reset for TTT: switch SGD to nesterov=True (lookahead gradient computes at θ-lr·v, more accurate in the ~6-step-per-chunk regime) and clear optimizer.state at each chunk boundary (removes stale velocity from the previous chunk's different distribution).

Combine exp_075's recurrence-block gradient equalization (rescale blocks 3-5 grad by 1/(num_loops+1)=1/3 after backward) with Nesterov momentum (nesterov=True) in TTT SGD, on top of exp_082's NLL-adaptive LR — both improvements were on separate lineage branches and have never been combined.

Replace standard CE with focal CE (γ=2) in TTT adaptation step: down-weight tokens the model predicts confidently (focal_w=(1-p_t)^2 ≈ 0) and concentrate gradient signal on hard tokens that inflate BPB most, making each TTT step more efficient per chunk.

Extend warmdown_frac from 0.72→0.76: with min_lr=0.15 floor both schedules reach the floor at ~533s, but the shallower linear slope gives the EMA more time accumulating near-converged weights, better matching the hard-task WSD regime where longer decay fractions are optimal.

Revert exp_094's catastrophic per_token.clamp(max=5.0) and replace with F.cross_entropy label_smoothing=0.01: at vocab=8192 the clamp silenced all gradients at init (ln(8192)≈9>>5), and ε=0.01 micro-smoothing may marginally improve post-GPTQ calibration without measurable gradient distortion.

Split Muon WD within attention block by functional role: Q/K routing matrices get muon_wd_qk=0.075 (lower, needs more optimization freedom), V/O content matrices get muon_wd_vo=0.110 (higher, functions like MLP projection); analogous to the successful attn/MLP WD split (exp_013) but applied within attn itself.

Peri-LN (ICLR 2025, arxiv 2502.02732): add a parameter-free RMSNorm to each sublayer's OUTPUT before the residual add — sandwich-style y = x + scale * RMSNorm(Module(RMSNorm(x))) — bounding per-sublayer contribution magnitude in both sequential (layers 0-6) and parallel-residual (layers 7-10) modes.

Raise scalar_lr 0.02→0.03 (50% bump) on the AdamW group containing the bifurcated q_gain[H,2] (exp_030's biggest +Δ), attn_scale/mlp_scale, resid_mix, and skip_gates — an entirely untouched meta knob currently identical to matrix_lr=0.022, while modern muP/normalized-transformer recipes give scalar gains materially higher LR than matrices because they are few-parameter and need fast convergence in our undertrained 600s regime.

Split GPTQ clip sigmas: attention layers (c_q/c_k/c_v/proj) use attn_clip_sigmas=10.0 vs uniform 12.85, giving a finer int6 quantization grid for attn weights (few outliers beyond 10σ) while MLP layers keep 12.85 to preserve their wider-tail outliers.

Dynamic small→large batch schedule: use half-size batch (393k tokens) for the pre-warmdown phase (0–28% of training), then switch to full batch (786k tokens) at warmdown start; pre-compile both shapes during warmup to avoid mid-training recompile, yielding ~25% more optimizer steps early when critical batch size is small.

Three-phase batch schedule (quarter→half→full): add phase-0 with quarter-batch (196k tokens, 12 seqs/GPU) for the first 10% of training (0–60s), then phase-1 half-batch (28%) and full-batch (28–100%) as before; pre-compile the quarter-batch graph during warmup so no mid-training recompile overhead.

Three-phase batch schedule (quarter→half→full) v2: phase-0 quarter-batch (196k tokens) for first 10% of training, phase-1 half-batch (393k) for 10–28%, phase-2 full-batch (786k) from 28%; quarter-batch graph compiles naturally at step 0 (all 8 GPUs simultaneously, no NCCL risk) removing the 4th warmup pass that caused v1 crash.

Raise AdamW β₂ from 0.95→0.99 at warmdown start for tok_emb and scalar optimizer groups: stable second-moment estimates let the LR schedule—not gradient magnitude fluctuations—control step size during warmdown (arxiv 2508.01483: consistent improvements with higher β₂ during WSD cooldown).

Give skip_weights and skip_gates their own AdamW param group with weight_decay=0.0 (freed from adam_wd=0.02): WD was decaying these critical skip-connection parameters by ~80% over training (scalar_lr×adam_wd per step over ~4000 steps ≈ e^-1.6 factor), forcing the optimizer to constantly fight WD just to maintain useful skip magnitudes.

Lower muon_momentum_warmup_start 0.92 to 0.85 (canonical Modded-NanoGPT value): untouched in 116 trials despite peak and fraction being tuned; lower start means first ~10% of training has Muon updates 50% closer to on-policy gradient (1-0.85=0.15 vs 1-0.92=0.08), giving better basin selection in the highly-plastic 600s undertrained regime, then ramps to preserved peak 0.99 over the same 0.33 fraction.

Extend half-batch phase from 28% to 38% of training: keep 2-phase schedule (half→full) but with transition at 38% instead of 28%, giving 10% more of training (60s) with more optimizer steps while LR is still at 88% of peak.

Add PaLM-style z-loss (1e-4 * mean(logsumexp(logits)^2)) to the CE loss: penalizes the log-partition function magnitude, keeping logit scale well-conditioned and reducing the effective weight singular-value range needed — improving GPTQ int6 quantization fidelity.

Switch MLP activation from LeakyReLU(slope=0.5).square() to ReLU.square() (PrimerEZ / Haziza 2025 srelu): zero the negative tail entirely instead of leaking 0.25·z², inducing activation sparsity that may regularize the undertrained MLP. One-character edit (negative_slope 0.5→0.0 equivalent); identical shapes / dtypes / compile-graph topology / param count, so eval budget is unaffected.

Tighten TTT gradient clip from 1.0 to 0.5: motivated by the LaCT paper (arXiv:2505.23884) finding that sequential gradient accumulation in TTT causes magnitude explosion; halving the clip norm reduces per-step parameter drift, preventing overfitting to each chunk while matching the tighter 0.3 clip used during training.

Loss-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration: two-pass approach computes per-batch CE loss then re-accumulates Hessian with each batch weighted by its normalized loss, biasing GPTQ error compensation toward sequences where the model is most uncertain (highest BPB contribution).

Combined frequency+loss Hessian weighting: extend exp_130's loss-weighted calibration by also scaling activations for the top-100 most-frequent tokens by sqrt(2), so the Hessian simultaneously emphasizes high-uncertainty batches (loss weight) and frequent tokens (freq weight), two complementary orthogonal signals for GPTQ error compensation.

Add within-word BPE position embeddings: zero-init nn.Embedding(8, 512) injected after input RMSNorm using a causal cummax scan over SP8192 word-start flags (persistent=False LUT, not in artifact), giving the model explicit intra-word token position without future leakage.

Raise tied_embed_lr 0.03→0.04 (an axis untouched in 135 trials): with tied embeddings the tok_emb param receives output-projection (softmax-side) gradients which dominate the input-embedding gradients — currently the tied_embed_lr/matrix_lr ratio of 1.36 is closer to μP-style than to the √d≈22.6 ratio recommended by arxiv 2506.15025 for large-vocab models, so a +33% bump samples the theoretically-favored direction while staying small-radius (still 15× below the untied default of 0.6).

Implement Muon-VS (Variance-Adaptive Muon, arxiv 2601.14603): add per-element variance scaling of the Nesterov momentum before Newton-Schulz orthogonalization using the existing muon_beta2=0.95 EMA buffer, dampening high-variance gradient directions and amplifying low-variance ones for more efficient orthogonal updates.

Hard-token loss weighting: replace uniform CE mean with per-token loss-proportional weights (w = per_tok / mean_per_tok, detached), computing mean(per_tok²)/mean(per_tok) — upweights hard tokens ~proportionally to their difficulty, concentrating gradient on positions where the model errs most.

Fuse per-parameter TTT gradient AllReduce (150+ individual NCCL calls) into a single float32 flat-buffer AllReduce per step to eliminate kernel-launch overhead (~15ms/step → ~14s saved), then use the freed eval budget to add a 4th TTT epoch (ttt_epochs 3→4, +33% adaptation per chunk at ~1ms cost).

Add per-head sigmoid output gate on attention (NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper, arXiv:2505.06708): apply gate=sigmoid(α·||y||₂ + β) per head with α=0/β=+4 init (transparent at step-0) between SDPA(+XSA) and proj, introducing non-linearity on the low-rank softmax mapping plus output-magnitude-dependent sparse gating to mitigate attention-sink at near-zero param/byte cost (176 scalars total).

Set scalar_wd=0.0 (down from adam_wd=0.02) for optimizer_scalar: attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, and q_gain are initialized at non-zero target values (1.0, 1.0, [1.0,0], 5.25) yet WD constantly pulls them toward zero, creating optimization friction that forces gradients to fight the WD force rather than learning the optimal scale.

Replace cumulative NLL running-average reference in TTT adaptive LR with EMA (decay=0.95, α=0.05): compares each chunk's difficulty to a ~20-chunk local window instead of all prior chunks, making the cosine LR scaling more responsive to FineWeb's domain-shift structure.

Reduce GPTQ block_size 128→64 and damp_factor 0.01→0.005: finer inter-block error propagation plus less Hessian over-regularization should improve quantization fidelity without changing artifact size.

MiLe Loss (arXiv 2310.19531): replace static BPE-rank weighting with dynamic entropy-weighted CE — weight_i = sqrt(1 + H(p_i)) / mean, where H(p_i) is Shannon entropy of model's prediction at each position; early training is near-uniform (undertrained model → high entropy everywhere), warmdown increasingly down-weights confident easy tokens and focuses gradient on remaining hard tokens.

Raise ttt_momentum 0.9→0.93 (untouched scalar axis in 142 trials): three prior trials (exp_031, exp_091, exp_138) all reset the cross-chunk SGD momentum buffer and all regressed +0.0005-0.0009, establishing that cross-chunk velocity carryover is beneficial; raising β to 0.93 extends velocity timescale 10→14 inner steps, amplifying the same beneficial smoothing without zeroing it.

Add per-dimension SmearAlpha: zero-init [model_dim=512] parameter that additively injects the previous position's normalized embedding into the current embedding (`x += smear_alpha * x_prev`), giving the model cheap causal bigram context in the input layer at zero initial cost (identity at init, ≤200 compressed bytes).

Apply gradient clipping only to AdamW param groups (embed+scalar) instead of all parameters: Muon's Newton-Schulz orthogonalization already normalizes update matrices to unit spectral norm, so the global pre-NS clip was distorting gradient directions entering the momentum buffer; removing the clip for Muon lets NS capture the true gradient distribution.
Fuse per-parameter TTT gradient AllReduce (150+ NCCL calls) into a single float32 flat-buffer AllReduce per step; revert ttt_epochs to 3 after exp_145 showed 4 epochs overruns budget by ~3.5s.

Add mid-warmdown shard reshuffle at frac=0.55: the 72%-long warmdown runs on a single fixed shard permutation set at frac=0.28; a second reshuffle midway through (frac=0.55, LR still at 62% of peak) gives fresh data orderings for the second half of warmdown, improving data diversity during convergence.

Bifurcated K-gain [num_kv_heads=4, 2] mirroring exp_030's Q-gain win on the K side: split K into RoPE/NoPE channels and apply per-kv-head learnable scale (init=1.0, step-0 identical) so each kv-head can independently sharpen the RoPE vs NoPE contribution to logits — a DOF that q_gain[H,2] cannot express because Q-side scaling is finer-grained per-q-head but identical across the 2 q-heads sharing each kv-head.

Lower embed_wd from 0.085 to 0.05: in the severely undertrained 10-minute regime with tiny embed init (std=0.005) and low tied_embed_lr=0.03, the current WD over-constrains the embedding, preventing it from learning distinctive token representations; reducing WD by ~41% lets embeddings grow more expressive while logit_softcap=30.0 prevents overconfidence.

Lower embed_wd further from 0.05 to 0.0: exp_157 confirmed lower embed WD improves val_bpb; with WD=0 the embedding is governed purely by gradient signal (dominated by output projection), consistent with scalar_wd=0.0 already working fine; the output projection gradient is strong enough to self-regularize without explicit WD shrinkage.

Double TTT chunk size from 32K to 64K tokens: same total TTT compute (half as many gradient steps, each with 2× the sequences per rank), but more coherent per-chunk gradient signal and half the chunk-level AllReduce overhead. Hypothesis: fewer but larger-batch gradient steps improve TTT convergence vs noisier 16-seq steps at 32K.

Raise AdamW BETA1 from 0.9 to 0.95 — the only major AdamW hyperparameter untouched in 161 trials. The KellerJordan/modded-nanogpt 320-experiment sweep at d20 found x0_beta1=0.96 optimal vs the legacy 0.9 default; we adopt a conservative 0.95 step toward that finding. Mechanism: longer momentum half-life (10→20 steps) better averages the asymmetric gradient pattern on tied tok_emb (sparse rare-token input grads + dense output-projection grads), and smooths the bifurcated q_gain[H,2] / scalar group whose per-batch gradients fluctuate with token mix; the recently-kept embed_wd reduction (0.085→0.05, exp_157) reduced one source of effective regularization on tok_emb, so additional momentum-side smoothing fills the gap without raising the LR axis itself.

Replace fixed sigma-based row scale (12.85*std/63) with per-row MSE-optimal percentile selection: try 5 clip percentiles [0.999, 0.9995, 0.9999, 0.99999, 1.0] per row and pick the one minimizing reconstruction MSE, giving ~4x finer quantization grid for the bulk of weights vs current near-no-clipping approach.

Add per-dimension causal bigram mixing (bigram_mix=[model_dim=512], zero-init): shifts embeddings right-by-1 and element-wise mixes the previous token's embedding into the current before RMSNorm, enriching x0 with bigram context that propagates through all 4 decoder layers via resid_mix injection — near-zero artifact cost (~1KB passthrough float16).

Replace failed TOP hinge loss with z-loss (α=1e-4 × mean(log²Σexp(logits))): removes exp_161's harmful auxiliary and adds PaLM/T5-style partition-function regularization that sharpens predictions throughout training without any scheduling.

Stochastic depth with linear depth scaling (max_rate=0.1): layer i drops its full residual delta with prob i/10 × 0.1, forcing each layer to independently develop useful representations rather than co-adapting with neighbors; scaled by 1/keep_prob to preserve expected residual magnitude during training.

Quarter-batch phase 1 (196k tokens, 12 seqs/GPU) for 0–28% of training: same 2-shape compilation pattern as exp_109 (no 3-shape crash risk), but delivers 4× more optimizer steps at peak LR vs full batch instead of 2×, maximizing exploration before warmdown convergence.

Muon momentum cooldown 0.99→0.95 after warmup: once the momentum warmup completes at 33% of training, linearly decay Muon momentum from 0.99 to 0.95 over the remaining 67%, reducing momentum inertia during the convergence phase so NS-orthogonalized updates track local gradients more precisely during warmdown.

Muon momentum cooldown 0.99→0.90 (deeper): extend exp_176's winning cooldown endpoint from 0.95 to 0.90, reducing Muon's effective window from ~20 to ~10 steps by end of training for even more precise gradient tracking during late warmdown convergence.

Raise AdamW BETA2 from 0.95 to 0.98 — untouched in 179 trials (only beta1 tested at exp_169, adam_eps at exp_135). Current beta1=beta2=0.95 collapses m and v EMA half-lives to ~14 steps, defeating Adam's variance-adaptation purpose; restoring m << v separation (v half-life ~35 steps) gives more stable per-row effective LR on tied tok_emb (8192×512) and on the AdamW scalar group (q_gain[H,2], attn_scale, mlp_scale, resid_mix, skip_gates) especially across the 72% warmdown. Backed by the Fantastic Pretraining Optimizers paper (arxiv 2509.02046) using beta2=0.98 as 130M baseline.

V-RMSNorm symmetric to existing Q/K norm (HybridNorm 2503.04598, 2025): add F.rms_norm to V after c_v projection so QKV are all unit-norm per head. Parameter-free, single extra rms_norm over Hkv=4 vs Q's Hk=8.

Add learnable output_shift [model_dim=512] zero-init, applied to x before the logit projection: F.linear(x+shift, W) = raw_logits + W@shift, giving a per-token logit bias via the embedding rows at only 512 params (2KB) to learn frequency priors explicitly without the 32KB cost of [vocab_size] bias.

Raise ttt_lr from 0.005 to 0.008: with AdamW(beta1=0) the effective per-step update is ~lr×sign(gradient), so total weight movement per chunk = 3 steps × lr; at 0.005 the model moves only 1.5% of typical weight magnitude, likely underfitting the adaptation budget — increasing to 0.008 gives 2.4% movement while keeping max effective LR (with nll_scale=2.0 and cosine=1.0) at 0.016, still conservative.

Delay Muon momentum cooldown start from frac=0.33 to frac=0.70: hold momentum at peak 0.99 during the majority of warmdown (33-70%), then do a faster 0.99→0.95 cooldown only in the final 30%, letting high momentum escape poor basins despite declining LR before switching to precise gradient tracking at convergence.

Lower muon_wd_mlp from 0.115 to 0.095 to equalize MLP and attention weight decay: in the 10-minute undertrained regime MLP is capacity-constrained by time not overfitting, and its 5.2x shrinkage-to-LR ratio vs attn's 4.3x unnecessarily limits MLP expressiveness; also isolating the WD split that was conflated with ttt_epochs change in exp_013.

Byte-weighted CE training loss: weight each token's NLL by its actual encoded byte count (base_bytes + leading_space from SP LUTs), normalized per batch — directly aligning the training CE objective with BPB = sum(NLL_t)/sum(bytes_t), so the optimizer prioritizes multi-byte subwords (like ' the'=4B, 'tion'=4B) over single-byte tokens (',','a') proportionally to their BPB contribution.

Raise muon_momentum_warmup_start 0.92→0.95: untouched higher-side bracket of an axis where exp_125 already showed lower (0.85) hurts. With current batch curriculum (half-size phase 1, frac 0–0.28) the small-batch noisy gradient phase coincides exactly with the lowest Muon momentum during warmup ramp — raising the start narrows ramp range from 0.92→0.99 (7pp) to 0.95→0.99 (3pp), increasing gradient averaging during the noisier small-batch phase without delaying the warmup peak; cooldown logic (start=warmup-end at frac=0.33, end=0.95) is unchanged so late-training dynamics are identical.

Replace additive output_shift (exp_187, +0.000170 BPB penalty) with multiplicative output_scale [model_dim=512] ones-init: x_scaled = x * output_scale before the tied embedding projection, giving a learned per-dimension diagonal reweighting of logit similarities that decouples which embedding dimensions matter for prediction vs. input lookup — exp_089 tried this technique but hit eval_budget_overrun without producing a valid bpb; now retried on the current (eval-stable) codebase.
test

Mask first 64 positions from training loss to align gradient with stride-64 sliding-window eval: positions 0–63 per sequence have no prior context (cold-start) while every evaluated token (after chunk 0) has ≥64 tokens of context; slicing logits/targets at [:, 64:] is a static shape change compile-safe.

RWKV-V5+ token shift on attention input: per-channel learnable lerp between RMSNormed activations and their 1-step causal shift, applied in every Block. mu init=0 → step-0 identical; gives each layer absolute prev-token info as a structural complement to RoPE's relative-position bias on Q/K, and looped layers 3-5 apply the shift 3× per forward on an evolving residual to expand effective receptive field at no extra compute.

Add per-dimension causal bigram mixing (bigram_mix=[512], zero-init): uses F.pad to shift embeddings right-by-1, then adds bigram_mix.to(dtype) * prev before RMSNorm, enriching x0 with bigram context that propagates through all decoder layers via resid_mix — ~1KB artifact cost.

Lower enable_looping_at 0.35→0.28 to align loop activation with the batch-switch transition: an UNTOUCHED meta axis in 170 trials. Currently the 7% gap (0.28–0.35) trains a "full-batch + no-looping" graph that is NEVER used at eval (eval uses looped graph 3), so those steps train a config the model is never deployed in. Aligning eliminates the wasteful intermediate phase, giving 7% more wallclock (~42s) to eval-config training. Both transitions now happen on the same step but graph 3 (full-batch+looping) is already precompiled in warmup so there is zero recompile cost. Mechanically supported by Sparse Growing Transformer (arxiv 2603.23998) "progressively extends recurrence" framing — the deep variant gets more time to converge in the regime it will be evaluated in.

Switch TTT optimizer from SGD+momentum to AdamW(betas=(0.0,0.9), weight_decay=0): beta1=0 eliminates stale cross-chunk momentum while beta2=0.9 builds per-parameter variance estimates across chunks, giving SignSGD-like normalization in early chunk steps — handles heterogeneous quantization error across layers better than flat SGD LR.

Remove exp_172's harmful z-loss and add label smoothing ε=0.02: z-loss at 1e-4 penalizes the log-partition function regardless of correctness (exp_127, exp_172 both hurt), while label_smoothing=0.02 only softens the hard-target gradient by 2% — a clean test from exp_157's baseline since exp_104's ε=0.01 result was confounded by simultaneously reverting a catastrophic per-token clamp.

MSE-optimal percentile scale selection (exp_170 approach) for all layers, plus int5 for MLP down-projection layers: keeps the ~0.008 BPB quality gain from finer quantization grids on attention/mlp-fc (int6-percentile) while switching mlp.proj to int5-percentile (still 2.5× finer than original sigma=12.85 scheme) to reduce brotli artifact size and clear the 16 MB cap.

Split zero-init output projections (attn.proj + mlp.proj) into a dedicated Muon group with muon_wd_proj=0.05, down from 0.095/0.115: zero-init params must grow from zero during training and high WD continuously resists that growth — same mechanism as exp_157's embed_wd reduction for tiny-init embeddings.

Stratified shard sampling: replace proportional-remaining shard selection with round-robin via shuffled shard permutation each batch step, guaranteeing all shards contribute equally to every batch (vs proportional which statistically skips ~12% of shards per step with 12 shards and 24 seqs).

Add SLOT-style logit-bias correction to TTT scoring: a [vocab_size=8192] float32 bias is zero-initialized, analytically updated each chunk via the CE gradient (model_pred_freq - target_freq, LR=0.5) using already-computed forward-pass logits (no extra backward), then applied at scoring of subsequent chunks — captures token-frequency distribution shift complementary to weight-level TTT adaptation.

Int4+percentile for all matrix layers: use MSE-optimal percentile scale selection (exp_170 approach) with int4 quantization (clip_range=7, values in [-7,7]) instead of int6+sigma. Int4+percentile step size ≈ 2.5σ/7 = 0.36σ is marginally finer than int6+sigma (12.85σ/31 = 0.41σ), so quality should match or beat baseline, while 15 distinct values vs 63 allows brotli to compress the artifact back under 16 MB.

Combined freq+loss GPTQ Hessian weighting: multiply existing per-token loss weights (exp_130) by per-token frequency weights sqrt(freq[x_t]/mean_freq), biasing Hessian accumulation toward tokens that are both uncertain (high loss) AND frequent (high BPB impact), targeting the intersection of the two previously separate gains.

Combined freq+loss GPTQ Hessian weighting: multiply existing per-token loss weights by per-token frequency weights sqrt(freq[x_t]/mean_freq) — fix GPU→CPU bug from exp_192 crash, same mathematical intent: bias Hessian toward tokens that are both uncertain (high loss) AND frequent (high BPB impact).

Add floor of max(0.3,...) to cosine TTT LR decay: prevents near-zero adaptation on late validation chunks where domain shift may demand more gradient signal, consistent with 'LR decay wastes challenging late data' findings (arxiv 2511.18903).

Upgrade GPTQ Hessian calibration from per-batch loss weighting (exp_130) to per-token loss weighting: each token position's activation is scaled by sqrt(loss_t/mean_loss) so H = sum_t (loss_t/mean_loss)*x_t*x_t^T, concentrating calibration on positions where quantization error most degrades BPB.

Raise tied_embed_init_std 0.005→0.020: an untouched meta axis in 151 trials. Tied tok_emb doubles as output projection so init logit std = σ·√d = σ·22.6; current 0.005 → 0.113 (near-uniform softmax for hundreds of steps wasting CE-grad signal); 0.020 → 0.45 (meaningfully non-uniform softmax from step 1, well below logit_softcap=30 so no saturation), giving downstream layers informative gradients earlier in the 600s undertrained regime.

Token Order Prediction (TOP, arXiv 2508.19228) auxiliary pairwise hinge loss: at each position i, apply α=0.1·mean(relu(logit[t+2]−logit[t+1])) so the existing logit at position i is penalized when it ranks the 2-ahead token above the immediate next token; replaces discarded MiLe Loss with a sparse ranking constraint that uses zero new parameters and is fullgraph=True compatible.

Double TTT chunk size 32768→65536: same total training FLOPs (halved chunk count × doubled seqs/chunk) but 2× larger effective batch per optimizer step, giving lower gradient variance and halving AllReduce overhead (~210→105 total calls).

Move batch_phase1_frac from 0.28 to 0.22: creates a 6%-window (22–28% of training) where full batch runs at peak LR before warmdown starts, separating the exploration phase (small-batch high-variance) from a consolidation phase (full-batch peak-LR) before the long decay.

Add 8 learnable frequency-bin logit calibration scalars (zero-init) applied post-softcap: BPE token IDs roughly correlate with subword frequency, so per-bin biases let the model explicitly calibrate its output logit distribution by token frequency class without any new weight matrices.

Extend EMA decay from 0.9965→0.9985 (effective window ~286→667 steps): exp_085 showed shortening the window (0.9965→0.99, ~100 steps) badly hurt (Δ=+0.003), implying the optimal window is longer than current; at 10-15 steps/sec on 8 GPUs the flat LR-floor tail spans ~430-650 steps, so the current 286-step window only covers ~50-65% of it—extending to 667 steps achieves full flat-tail averaging for more uniform weight consolidation at convergence.

Soft sequential injection in parallel-residual layers (7-10): add per-channel learnable parameter parallel_attn_inject [dim=512] to each parallel block, additively injecting attn_out into the MLP input (mlp_in = x_in + inject * attn_out, then mlp_norm). Init=0 → step-0 numerically identical to pure parallel residual; non-zero values smoothly interpolate toward sequential pre-norm where MLP attends to attn output. 4 layers × 512 fp32 = 8 KB raw → float16 passthrough ≈ 4 KB; brotli further. Untried structural DOF — opens up inter-sublayer information flow that exp_137's blanket XSA-disable on parallel layers couldn't access.

Replace standard CE with focal loss (γ=2) in TTT inner training loop: focal_w=(1-p_t)^2 up-weights hard tokens (domain-specific vocabulary the model currently mispredicts) and suppresses easy tokens, concentrating adaptation signal where it matters most.

Add learnable per-feature input embedding scale [model_dim=512], ones-init, applied after functional RMSNorm on token embeddings; decouples per-dimension input sensitivity from the tied output projection and lets the model emphasize useful embedding directions without adding vocab-sized params.

Delay LR warmdown start from 28% to 35% (warmdown_frac 0.72→0.65): with exp_109's batch curriculum, batch switches from half→full at exactly t=28%=warmdown start, so the model never sees full-batch training at peak LR; separating the transitions by 7% (~42s) lets the optimizer exploit stable full-batch gradients before entering convergence mode.

Restore exp_092's validated TTT improvements onto exp_109: add nesterov=True to TTT SGD and recurrence-block gradient equalization (rescale blocks 3-5 grad by 1/(num_loops+1)=1/3 after backward), which were lost when the curr specialist built exp_109 from exp_092's snapshot without carrying forward these TTT gains.

Extend half-batch phase from 28% to 34% of training: transition stays below enable_looping_at=0.35 so looping always activates with the pre-compiled full-batch shape, avoiding the uncompiled [half-batch+looping] shape that caused exp_126's crash.

Lower adam_eps 1e-8→1e-10: untouched in 127 trials. With tied tok_emb (vocab=8192), per-row gradients for moderately-rare tokens are ~1e-5 to 1e-4, so v~1e-10 to 1e-8 is comparable to eps=1e-8 — the eps floor suppresses Adam's adaptive normalization on long-tail vocab rows. Lowering to 1e-10 lets sqrt(v) dominate even for rows with grad above ~1e-6, restoring per-row effective LR for the rare-token rows that disproportionately impact BPB.

Token-level data augmentation: randomly replace 1% of input tokens with uniform-random vocab tokens during training, forcing the model to use broader context rather than relying on immediate-predecessor copying, potentially improving robustness to small perturbations analogous to GPTQ quantization noise.

Disable XSA on parallel-residual decoder layers (7-10): XSA's self-V-projection subtraction is an inductive bias against degenerate attention-output redundancy in the *sequential* pre-norm residual stream; in the GPT-J-style parallel-residual topology (layers 7-10) attn and MLP both write into x_in independently rather than chained, so XSA's residual-stream rationale is absent. Compute-negative (saves 4 normalize+reduction passes/forward → buys eval-budget headroom against the 600s cap that the prior 5 arch trials kept overrunning); step-0 bit-identical because proj._zero_init=True forces attn_out=0.

Reset SGD momentum buffer between TTT chunks: FineWeb validation spans diverse topics across consecutive 32K-token chunks, so stale inter-chunk velocity from chunk k misguides the first few gradient steps of chunk k+1; zeroing the buffer before each chunk preserves within-chunk momentum accumulation while eliminating cross-chunk contamination.

BPE-rank frequency-stratified loss weighting: up-weight rare tokens (higher vocab IDs) by up to 15% using normalized BPE rank as a frequency proxy (weight = 1 + 0.15 * rank, mean=1), compensating for their systematic underrepresentation in training batches vs their equal per-occurrence contribution to BPB.

Switch warmdown LR shape from linear to sqrt: replace (1-frac)/warmdown_frac with sqrt((1-frac)/warmdown_frac), keeping LR higher through most of warmdown (e.g. at 50% through: 0.707 vs 0.5) and deferring the min_lr floor hit from ~535s to ~590s, giving more training time at productive learning rates.

Halve TTT chunk size from 32K to 16K tokens: 2× more adaptation events with the same total budget, making nll_scale more responsive to FineWeb's domain-shift structure; the cosine LR schedule normalizes by num_chunks so per-token LR envelope is identical.

Add learnable logit_bias [vocab_size=8192] zero-init vector, added inside the softcap before tanh, to give the model a dedicated unigram-prior channel disentangled from the tied embedding's semantic role (per "A Natural Bias for Language Generation Models", ACL 2023).

Raise V/O WD to match MLP (muon_wd_vo 0.110→0.115) and restore Q/K WD to attn baseline (muon_wd_qk 0.075→0.095): V/O matrices perform content extraction like MLP and should share MLP-level regularization, while Q/K routing matrices keep baseline WD.

Replace Nesterov-SGD TTT inner optimizer with Muon-style updates: NS5-orthogonalized gradient (using existing zeropower_via_newtonschulz5 in bfloat16 to reuse compiled graph) + Nesterov momentum buffer carried across chunks; LR scaled by 0.25× to match SGD update magnitude. Motivated by LaCT (arxiv 2505.23884) showing Muon consistently outperforms SGD momentum for chunk-wise TTT.

Lower embed_wd 0.085→0.05 on current best 092: exp_083 showed -0.000150 improvement over its parent (064) with this exact change but lost a sibling race to 082 by +0.000009 (pure noise); current best 092 inherits 082's embed_wd=0.085, so combining is genuinely untested. Mechanism (per arxiv 2603.26663) — tied tok_emb/lm_head behaves as output projection because output gradients dominate early; in our 600s undertrained regime WD=0.085 over-regularizes a still-converging output map. Independent of 082/092's TTT-side improvements.

Depth-recurrence-aware q_gain: promote per-head bifurcated q_gain from [H,2] to [num_iters,H,2] so each of the 3 calls per loop layer (3,4,5) gets its own RoPE/NoPE channel scale, threaded as a Python int kwarg through forward (zero compute, ~352 fp32 params, step-0 numerically identical to current).

Reverse-position gentle CE weighting (1.1→0.9 linspace, mean=1.0): early sequence positions get 10% more gradient weight and late positions 10% less — the opposite direction from exp_057 (which tried 0.75→1.25) — to emphasise context-independent document-structure signal at sequence starts.

Post-GPTQ Hessian-weighted optimal scale refinement: after SDClip GPTQ returns (Q, s_heuristic), recompute per-row scale as s_opt[r] = sum_j(H_jj * Q[r,j] * W[r,j]) / sum_j(H_jj * Q[r,j]^2), minimizing the Hessian-weighted reconstruction error for the given Q values. Zero artifact size impact since Q shape and s dtype/shape are unchanged.

Learnable logit_bias with 512 frequency buckets (vocab_size//16, zero-init), expanded via repeat_interleave(16) and added inside the softcap — gives a coarse unigram-prior channel disentangled from the tied embedding, while fitting within the artifact budget (~875 compressed bytes vs 14KB for the full-vocab version).

Fix eval_budget_overrun from exp_114 by replacing compiled zeropower_via_newtonschulz5 with non-compiled inline _ttt_ns5 (3 steps, eager) in the TTT Muon update, eliminating torch.compile recompilation overhead (~8-10s) that caused the 3.6s budget overrun.

Restore muon_momentum_warmup_fraction 0.33→0.45 in exp_082 baseline: the eval specialist submitted exp_082 from a stale workdir that did not inherit exp_064's validated 0.45 setting, so the current best runs Muon with only 198s of momentum warmup instead of the 270s shown to be optimal by exp_064 (Δ=-0.000038 on prior lineage).

Add MLP hidden dropout (p=0.02) applied after LeakyReLU²-square activation before output projection: first true dropout mechanism in this run (exp_036 was stochastic depth), zero→nonzero qualitative change; disabled during eval/TTT via model.eval() → training=False, so only training dynamics are affected while quantization/scoring are clean.

Headwise query-dependent sigmoid output gate (Qwen NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper, arxiv 2505.06708) on attention, restricted to the 3 recurrence-loop blocks (layers 3,4,5). Gate logits ride free on extended c_q output (no separate matmul); identity-init via gate_bias=5.0 (sigmoid≈0.993); applied element-wise per head between SDPA and o_proj. Recurrence amplifies the effect (each gated block traversed 3× per forward pass).

At warmdown start (frac=0.28), reset all per-rank shard orderings with fresh random phases and permutations so the 432s LR-decay convergence phase operates on a fully re-randomized data schedule rather than the tail of the initial epoch's permutation.

Raise grad_clip_norm 0.3→0.5: an untouched meta knob in this entire 90-trial run; 0.3 is unusually tight (modern recipes use 1.0 default), and since Muon row_normalize already bounds matrix updates, this clip primarily constrains AdamW scalar/embed updates including the bifurcated qk_gain [H,2] (exp_030's biggest recent gain) — loosening lets scalar params take larger steps during peak-LR phase when grads are most informative.

Discriminative per-layer TTT learning rates: tok_emb×0.5, blocks 0-2×0.3, blocks 3-7×0.7, blocks 8-10+final_norm×1.0; combined with existing NLL-adaptive cosine scaling so upper layers (most document-specific) adapt more aggressively each chunk.

Pre-norm bigram injection: add previous token's raw embedding (scaled by learnable scalar bigram_alpha, zero-init) to the current token embedding BEFORE RMSNorm, so the normalized direction encodes (prev, cur) bigram pair geometry — differs from exp_068's post-norm normalized-prev injection by baking the bigram signal into the normalized direction itself.

Lower embed_wd from 0.085 to 0.05 on tied tok_emb/lm_head: a previously untouched meta knob; the Power Lines paper (arxiv 2505.13738) shows optimal WD scales inversely with model size at fixed tokens-per-parameter, and our 600s budget is heavily undertrained, so the embedding (which doubles as output projection due to tying) is being over-regularized at 0.085.

Lower ema_decay from 0.9965 to 0.99 (EMA window ~100 steps ≈ 2.5% of ~4000-step run), focusing weight averaging on the LR-floor convergence phase; LAWA paper (arxiv 2306.03241) shows ~1% window is optimal. Also restores linear warmdown (reverting exp_073 cosine).

Replace per-shard random permutation with Weyl (golden-ratio) quasi-random permutation cycled across resets: each shard reset starts at a different offset in the golden-ratio sequence, ensuring maximally uniform positional coverage even when training terminates mid-epoch.

Lower Muon WD by 0.65× for recurrence loop blocks (3-5) only: loop blocks get 3× gradient per forward pass so their effective gradient-to-WD ratio is already 3× that of non-loop blocks; reducing their WD (attn 0.095→0.062, mlp 0.115→0.075) lets them find sharper minima without WD opposing the strong accumulated gradient signal.
Value-residual learning (ResFormer): per-kv-head lambda mixes each layer V toward layer-0 V. Init=0 so step-0 identical to baseline.

Revert label_smoothing (exp_056 regression) + split scalar AdamW into WD=0 for pure norm/bias params and adam_wd=0.02 for control tensors (q_gain, skip_weight, attn_scale), following standard practice of excluding LayerNorm/bias from weight decay to prevent artificial pull toward zero.

Add learnable bigram shortcut: bigram_alpha (scalar, zero-init) injects the previous-token normalized embedding into the current position, giving the first layers an explicit causal bigram signal and freeing MLP capacity from bigram statistics per Bigram Subnetworks NeurIPS 2025.

MiLe Loss (ICLR 2025): replace flat CE with entropy-weighted CE — per-token loss scaled by (1+H(p))/mean(1+H(p)) where H(p)=−∑p·logp is the model's predicted-distribution entropy (stop-grad), up-weighting positions with genuine multi-token ambiguity while down-weighting already-confident predictions.

Skip non-metric eval_val_sliding (saves ~100s) by defaulting sliding_window_enabled=0, decouple TTT from that flag, and restore ttt_epochs=3 (original baseline value, forced to 2 in exp_014 to clear budget); the freed eval budget more than covers the extra TTT adaptation epoch cost (~4% overhead).
Reduce GPTQ Hessian damping from 1% to 0.5% of H.diag().mean(): lower regularization bias in the Cholesky H-inverse yields more accurate per-column error compensation, at zero compute overhead.

Equalize TTT gradient magnitudes for recurrence blocks 3-5: they execute (num_loops+1)=3× per forward pass so their.grad accumulates 3× the magnitude of once-executed blocks; rescaling by 1/3 after backward() gives uniform effective TTT learning rate across all 11 blocks.

Hierarchical sliding-window attention on early encoder layers 1, 2 (window=512 of seq_len=2048) via FA3 window_size; all other layers stay full causal. Zero new params; biases early-encoder receptive field toward local n-gram aggregation while leaving the recurrence (layers 3-5) and decoder (6-10) globally attentive. Layer 0 keeps full attention to preserve the attention-sink mechanism.

Replace SGD with AdamW (betas=(0.9,0.95), wd=0) in TTT inner loop: AdamW's second-moment normalization automatically equalizes per-parameter step sizes across heterogeneous gradient scales (embedding vs deep MLP), generalizing the manual recurrence-block gradient scaling from exp_075 to all layer types.

Hierarchical sliding-window attention on early encoder layers 1, 2 (window=512 of seq_len=2048) via FA3 window_size; layer 0 + recurrence + decoder stay full causal. Zero new params; biases early-encoder receptive field toward local n-gram aggregation. Re-submit of #078 with SDPA fallback fixed (smoke ignores window_size — zero-param edit, artifact size unaffected) plus FA3-signature probe so an older flash_attn_interface gracefully degrades to full attention.

Add learnable per-dimension output scale (output_dim_scale, [model_dim=512], init=1) applied after final_norm but before the tied-embedding logit projection, learning a diagonal metric in the output space to reweight which embedding dimensions matter most for next-token prediction — unlike exp_058's input-side scale (cancelled by input RMSNorm), this scale directly modulates the logit similarity computation.

Per-token loss clipping at 5.0 nats: replace mean CE with mean(clamp(per_token_CE, max=5.0)), removing gradient from positions where the model gives <0.7% probability to the correct token, so the optimizer focuses on achievable improvements rather than noise-dominated impossible positions.

Split attention vs MLP clip sigmas: attention layers (c_q/c_k/c_v/proj) use attn_clip_sigmas=10.0 instead of the uniform 12.85, giving 22% finer int6 quantization grid for attn weights that have few outliers beyond 10σ, while MLP layers stay at 12.85 where the wider range is justified by exp_062's regression when MLP was widened to 15.0.
Value-residual learning (ResFormer): per-kv-head lambda mixes each layer V toward layer-0 V. Init=0 so step-0 identical to baseline. Always-apply formulation so v_lambda is in autograd graph for DDP.

Add label smoothing ε=0.1 to training cross-entropy loss (F.cross_entropy label_smoothing=0.1): prevents overconfident predictions, encourages distributed representations, and nudges weights toward lower-magnitude solutions that quantize more accurately under int6 GPTQ.

Position-weighted training CE: tokens at later positions in each 2048-token sequence get more gradient weight (linspace 0.75→1.25, mean=1.0), concentrating training signal on context-rich predictions that align with the sliding-window eval scoring pattern.

Add learnable affine scale (emb_norm_weight, [model_dim=512], init=1) applied after embedding RMSNorm — making it a standard affine RMSNorm at the input layer so the model can amplify/suppress per-dimension embedding signal.

Apply eval_temperature=0.97 sharpening to logits at scoring time in both eval_val_sliding and eval_val_ttt: GPTQ int6 quantization adds weight noise that flattens the logit distribution (underconfidence), and T=0.97 partially corrects this calibration drift at zero extra compute cost.

Add per-head learnable XSA strength scalar (init=1.0, [num_heads]=[8] per layer) so each head can independently attenuate/amplify the exclusive-self-attention self-projection subtraction; analogous to the q_gain bifurcation (exp_030 keep) but on the XSA mechanism.

Frozen-encoder TTT: run encoder blocks {0,1,2} under torch.no_grad() then detach at enc/dec boundary so backward only propagates through decoder blocks (9 of 17 invocations), saving ~31% of per-step backward compute and preventing encoder overfitting to chunk noise.

Byte-length-weighted training loss: weight each token's NLL by base_bytes_lut (clamped to ≥1, normalized to mean=1), aligning training gradient allocation with the BPB metric which weights by byte span.
Byte-length-weighted training loss (v2): manual reduction="none" + gather + weighted sum, fixing fullgraph=True incompatibility with F.cross_entropy(weight=...) from exp_063.

Per-block Muon WD split for recurrence layers (blocks 3-5): raise attn WD 0.095→0.120 and MLP WD 0.115→0.145 for the loop blocks that accumulate 3× gradient per optimizer step, while non-loop blocks keep existing WD values.

Add per-token learnable logit_bias [vocab_size=8192, init=0] to output logits before softcap, trained at tied_embed_lr with zero weight_decay — decouples token-frequency prior from the tied embedding direction so the model doesn't have to encode unigram statistics in embedding magnitudes.

Add z-loss regularization (coef=1e-4) to training CE: penalizes (log Z)^2 where Z=logsumexp(logits), complementing the existing logit softcap (which bounds individual logit range ≤30) by also constraining the partition function; encourages better-calibrated logit distributions and should reduce post-GPTQ int6 quantization error.

Prepend a single learnable [model_dim] sink token at relative position 0 of every forward pass (dropped before the LM head so input/output shape contract is preserved); init=0 makes step-0 a no-op, then the sink learns to absorb the attention-sink probability mass that otherwise concentrates on the first real token, providing a consistent attention anchor across train and sliding-window eval windows.
Revert exp_041 per-param-group LR to single SGD group (fixes eval overrun), add cosine LR floor at 0.5 so late-document chunks receive at least 50% of peak TTT LR instead of decaying toward zero.

Warmdown-phase gradient accumulation: during the 72% LR-decay window, run 2 micro-batches (each 96 seqs at shape 96×1024) per optimizer step instead of 1, doubling the effective batch to 1536 global seqs while keeping tensor shape fixed (no torch.compile recompile). Lower gradient variance (√2) during the precision-convergence phase.

Replace GPTQ row-scale std with Hessian-diagonal-weighted RMS: h_weights = H.diag()/sum normalizes input-feature importance, row_wrms = sqrt((W²) @ h_weights) gives activation-weighted effective magnitude — so rows with large weights on high-activation inputs get a scale that avoids clipping them while rows with large weights on low-activation inputs get finer quantization (AWQ principle applied to GPTQ row-scale).
Split GPTQ clip sigmas: MLP layers use mlp_clip_sigmas=15.0 (wider, preserves gate-row outliers) while attention layers keep matrix_clip_sigmas=12.85; embed unchanged at 20.0.

Per-chunk SGD momentum reset (optimizer.state.clear() at each chunk boundary) + cosine LR floor at 0.5*ttt_lr: prevents stale high-velocity from early high-LR chunks contaminating late-chunk updates, while floor ensures late chunks still adapt at 50% peak LR.

Fixed train_seq_len=1024 (eval_seq_len stays 2048): attention compute is 4x cheaper per step (O(T²)) → ~25-35% more gradient steps in 600s while total tokens/step is unchanged; rope_train_seq_len stays 2048 so no YaRN distortion at eval.

Apply frequency-weighted GPTQ Hessian calibration (top-100 most-frequent tokens get sqrt(2) activation scaling → 2× Hessian weight) on top of exp_014 (Muon WD split + ttt_epochs=2). exp_014 has plain unweighted GPTQ; this adds the orthogonal quant improvement that gave -0.0018 BPB over baseline in exp_002.
Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine warmdown (same warmdown_frac=0.72): gives ~13% more LR in early warmdown phase and sharper final decay, better exploiting the long 72% decay window.
Shorten Muon momentum warmup fraction 0.33 to 0.20

Stochastic depth: linear drop schedule (0% for block 0, 10% for block 10), applied as per-sample Bernoulli mask on the full block residual delta during training only. Forces early layers to learn independently and reduces co-adaptation.

Halve TTT chunk size 32768→16384 to double gradient step frequency at identical total backward-pass compute: same total seq-backward count (1220 per rank) but 2× more adaptation events, each closer in time to the scored windows.

Byte-weighted TTT adaptation loss: weight each token's NLL by its SP byte-length (base_bytes_lut, clamped ≥1, normalized) during TTT SGD updates, aligning the adaptation objective with the BPB metric that weights by bytes not tokens; also reverts exp_029 focal loss regression back to standard CE.

Fixed train_seq_len=1024 (eval_seq_len stays 2048): ~25-35% more gradient steps via 4x cheaper attention (O(T²)); training-loop eval_val now uses base_model (eager) to avoid DDP+fullgraph shape conflict that crashed exp_032.
Add cosine LR floor at 0.5*ttt_lr so the second half of TTT chunks (ci > num_chunks/2) adapt at 0.0025 instead of near-zero, preventing late-chunk adaptation collapse at zero eval time cost.
Flat TTT LR (no cosine decay) + ttt_epochs=2 to fix eval overrun: late chunks now get 2 full adaptation epochs vs ~0.3 effective epochs under cosine+3.

Add z-loss regularization (coef=1e-4, same as PaLM): penalizes (log Z)^2 where Z=logsumexp(logits), encouraging balanced logit distributions and improved quantization robustness at near-zero compute overhead.

Position-weighted training loss: linearly ramp per-token loss weight from 0.5 at position 0 to 1.5 at position 2047 (mean=1.0), concentrating gradient on late-context positions that the stride-64 sliding-window eval scores exclusively.
